universal_rubrics.name: Company Scale Similarity
universal_rubrics.description: Measures whether the candidate has experience working in organizations with scale comparable to the hiring company.

levels.poor: Candidate worked only in very small or unrelated scale environments compared to the target company.
levels.borderline: Candidate has limited exposure to larger systems or teams but not direct ownership at scale.
levels.good: Candidate worked in companies with similar scale in terms of engineering teams, customers, or product complexity.
levels.strong: Candidate demonstrated success operating at equal or larger scale organizations (large user base, enterprise systems, high traffic products).

universal_rubrics.name: Industry / Domain Alignment
universal_rubrics.description: Measures similarity between candidate industry experience and the job's product or technology domain.

levels.poor: No relevant industry or product domain experience.
levels.borderline: Adjacent domain experience but limited overlap with target industry.
levels.good: Relevant industry exposure with transferable product or technical knowledge.
levels.strong: Direct experience solving problems within the same industry or product category.

universal_rubrics.name: Execution Environment Fit
universal_rubrics.description: Measures whether the candidate has experience working in environments with similar speed, complexity, and iteration cycles.

levels.poor: Experience mainly in slow-moving environments with limited product or engineering iteration.
levels.borderline: Some exposure to agile or iterative environments but limited evidence of fast delivery.
levels.good: Regular experience delivering work in agile teams with frequent releases or product iterations.
levels.strong: Proven ability to operate in high-velocity environments with rapid experimentation and product delivery.

universal_rubrics.name: Culture Fit Signals
universal_rubrics.description: Signals indicating collaboration, ownership, communication, and leadership alignment with company culture.

levels.poor: Resume shows isolated responsibilities with minimal collaboration.
levels.borderline: Basic collaboration with limited cross-functional interaction.
levels.good: Demonstrates strong collaboration across teams such as engineering, product, and business.
levels.strong: Evidence of leadership, cross-functional influence, mentorship, or driving organizational alignment.

universal_rubrics.name: Career Trajectory & Seniority Alignment
universal_rubrics.description: Evaluates whether the candidate’s experience and growth trajectory matches the seniority expected in the JD.

levels.poor: Candidate experience significantly below expected role level.
levels.borderline: Some relevant experience but limited leadership or ownership signals.
levels.good: Experience aligns well with the role level and responsibilities.
levels.strong: Candidate demonstrates progression, leadership growth, and responsibilities exceeding role expectations.

product_manager.name: Product Strategy Ownership
product_manager.description: Measures the candidate’s ownership of product direction and strategic planning.

levels.poor: No ownership of product roadmap or strategic planning; mostly execution tasks.
levels.borderline: Some involvement in roadmap discussions but limited ownership.
levels.good: Owns roadmap decisions and prioritization aligned with business goals.
levels.strong: Defines multi-quarter product strategy with measurable business outcomes.

product_manager.name: Product Delivery & Execution
product_manager.description: Measures ability to deliver products or features successfully.

levels.poor: No evidence of shipping products or features.
levels.borderline: Participation in launches without clear ownership.
levels.good: Delivered multiple features or products with measurable adoption.
levels.strong: Led complex product launches with significant business impact.

product_manager.name: Data-Driven Product Thinking
product_manager.description: Measures ability to use data and experimentation in product decisions.

levels.poor: No product metrics or analytics mentioned.
levels.borderline: Tracks basic KPIs but limited evidence of data-driven decisions.
levels.good: Uses metrics to guide product decisions and feature prioritization.
levels.strong: Designs experiments and analytics frameworks improving product metrics.

product_manager.name: Customer & Market Insight
product_manager.description: Measures understanding of user needs and market opportunities.

levels.poor: No evidence of customer research or user insight.
levels.borderline: Mentions customer feedback but unclear product influence.
levels.good: Conducts user research influencing product decisions.
levels.strong: Transforms customer insights into product strategy or major features.

software_engineer.name: Technical Stack Alignment
software_engineer.description: Measures alignment between JD technology requirements and candidate experience.

levels.good: Hands-on experience with most technologies required by the JD.
levels.strong: Deep expertise with the tech stack including optimization or architecture work.

software_engineer.name: System Scale Experience
software_engineer.description: Measures experience with large-scale or production systems.

levels.poor: Only small projects or academic systems mentioned.
levels.good: Built or maintained production systems with measurable scale.
levels.strong: Designed distributed systems handling high traffic or large datasets.

software_engineer.name: Engineering Impact
software_engineer.description: Measures measurable improvements or technical contributions.

levels.strong: Delivered major technical improvements affecting large user bases.

data_analyst.name: Analytical Skills
data_analyst.description: Measures ability to analyze and interpret data.

levels.poor: Limited analytical tools or techniques used.
levels.good: Strong analytics using SQL, dashboards, and structured analysis.
levels.strong: Advanced analysis using statistical methods or predictive models.

data_analyst.name: Data Scale & Complexity
data_analyst.description: Measures the scale and complexity of data handled.

levels.poor: Small datasets or manual reporting tasks.
levels.good: Large dataset analysis and dashboard automation.
levels.strong: Handles large-scale analytics pipelines or complex data modeling.

data_analyst.name: Business Impact of Analysis
data_analyst.description: Measures how analytics influenced business decisions.

levels.borderline: Some analysis but unclear decision impact.
levels.good: Analysis improved product or operational decisions.
levels.strong: Insights directly influenced major strategic decisions.

ai_product_manager.name: AI Product Experience
ai_product_manager.description: Measures candidate exposure to AI/ML-driven products.

levels.poor: No AI or ML product exposure.
levels.good: Managed AI-enabled features or ML-powered products.
levels.strong: Led AI product initiatives delivering measurable outcomes.

ai_product_manager.name: AI Technical Fluency
ai_product_manager.description: Measures ability to work with ML teams and understand AI systems.

levels.poor: Minimal understanding of ML or AI technologies.
levels.good: Understands ML pipelines, model metrics, and training processes.
levels.strong: Deep collaboration with ML teams including model lifecycle decisions.

ai_product_manager.name: AI Product Impact
ai_product_manager.description: Measures measurable outcomes delivered by AI products.

levels.strong: AI initiatives significantly improved business or product outcomes.
